title: Best LLM model for Hermes
summary: Keith AI discusses token cost implications of using Claude with Hermes, highlighting that token optimization matters significantly for cost management. The video emphasizes how LLM model selection impacts operational expenses.
writeup: This short clip addresses the cost reality of running AI agents through third-party API services, using a concrete $64-per-week Claude API bill as the anchor example. The speaker explains that major providers including Anthropic, Gemini, and OpenAI subsidize usage on their monthly plans, making direct API calls significantly more expensive by comparison. A key constraint is noted: Claude, Gemini, and Kimi have moved to restrict Hermes agent connections outside their sanctioned surfaces, raising ban risk for workarounds.
key_points: Running a Hermes agent via direct Claude API access cost the speaker $64 in approximately one week, illustrating that token optimization is a practical financial concern, not just a performance one.

Anthropic, Gemini, and OpenAI each offer monthly subscription plans that effectively subsidize usage, making them substantially cheaper than equivalent direct API consumption for high-volume agent workloads.
Claude, Gemini, and Kimi have restricted or blocked third-party Hermes agent connections on their platforms, meaning users who attempt to route Hermes through these services risk account bans.
